commands.review_summary:
The PR makes the Claude/Cursor Extra usage menu-bar metric render provider cost as formatted currency text, with fallback to the existing percent display and focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a display enhancement for an existing menu-bar metric preference. Source inspection confirms current main has Extra usage percentage support, while the PR adds the missing currency text path.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is appropriate; the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real menu-bar proof before human merge review.

target.title: Show extra-usage spend text in menu bar for Claude/Cursor
target.body:
## Fixes

Fixes #1084 — show money spend in the macOS menu bar.

## Summary

- Reuse existing `MenuBarMetricPreference.extraUsage` for a display-only menu-bar enhancement
- When `.extraUsage` is selected and `snapshot.providerCost` is usable (`limit > 0`), render formatted spend text, for example `$12.34`
- When provider cost is missing or unusable, fall back to the existing percent display
- Scope is intentionally limited to Claude/Cursor through the existing preference support
- No provider fetcher, network, or UsageStore refresh changes

## Scope

- Display/menu-bar text only
- No provider fetcher changes
- No UsageStore refresh changes
- No OpenCode/OpenCodeGo changes
- No Kimi changes
- No MiniMax changes

## Validation

- `swift test --filter StatusItemExtraUsageMetricTests`
- `swift test --filter MenuBarMetricWindowResolverTests`
- `make check`
- `git diff --check`

## Runtime proof

Runtime proof for the display path:

- Branch: `codex/1084-menu-bar-extra-usage-spend-text`
- Commit: `43c8a2f`
- Tested actual PR display path for Cursor `.extraUsage`
- Input providerCost test fixture: `used = 12.34`, `limit = 100`, `currencyCode = "USD"`
- Output display text assertion: `$12.34`

The focused test calls:

`controller.menuBarDisplayText(for: .cursor, snapshot: snapshot)`

and asserts:

`#expect(displayText == "$12.34")`

So this verifies the actual PR display path returns `$12.34`, not just a mocked formatter call.

Proof command/output:

```text
$ git branch --show-current
codex/1084-menu-bar-extra-usage-spend-text

$ git log --oneline -1
43c8a2f Display extra-usage spend in menu bar

$ swift test --filter StatusItemExtraUsageMetricTests
...
◇ Test "menu bar extra usage preference shows currency spend text for cursor when provider cost exists" started.
✔ Test "menu bar extra usage preference shows currency spend text for cursor when provider cost exists" passed after 0.014 seconds.
◇ Test "menu bar extra usage preference shows currency spend text for claude when provider cost exists" started.
✔ Test "menu bar extra usage preference shows currency spend text for claude when provider cost exists" passed after 0.016 seconds.
◇ Test "menu bar extra usage preference falls back to existing percent text when provider cost is unavailable" started.
✔ Test "menu bar extra usage preference falls back to existing percent text when provider cost is unavailable" passed after 0.018 seconds.
✔ Suite StatusItemExtraUsageMetricTests passed after 0.153 seconds.
✔ Test run with 5 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.153 seconds.
```
target.author: yuxin-qiao
